anchor: ChatGPT Pro and Business frame GPT access in ChatGPT as unlimited or effectively unlimited, subject to guardrails.

anchor: OpenClaw recommends Node for stable gateways and does not recommend Bun for gateway installs.
note: The current CLI docs could not be parsed cleanly enough to restate the Node-versus-Bun guidance automatically. Methods: openclaw-cli:direct-fetch.

anchor: The strongest always-on OpenClaw pattern is one primary loopback-bound gateway with secure remote access and remote nodes where needed.
note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openclaw-multiple-gateways:direct-fetch, openclaw-remote-access:search-backed, openclaw-nodes:search-backed.

anchor: Cron and heartbeat serve different purposes and should be combined with explicit wake or continuation flows.
note: One or more official sources still require a real browser-backed or manual confirmation path before this anchor can be treated as fully current. Methods: openclaw-cron-vs-heartbeat:direct-fetch, openclaw-nodes:search-backed.

anchor: OpenClaw memory is Markdown-first, and Codex OAuth does not satisfy embeddings for memory search.
note: The latest memory docs still show Markdown-first memory and separate embedding requirements. Methods: openclaw-memory:direct-fetch.

anchor: OpenClaw supports QMD and local-first memory search backends.
note: The memory docs still advertise QMD and local-first search options. Methods: openclaw-memory:direct-fetch.

anchor: OpenClaw skills, plugins, and skill folders are supply-chain risk and must be treated as trusted or untrusted code accordingly.
note: OpenClaw security guidance still treats skills and plugins as trusted-code or supply-chain risk surfaces. Methods: openclaw-security:direct-fetch, openclaw-secrets:direct-fetch.

anchor: OpenClaw current docs expose strict config requirements, additive SecretRefs, and a first-class PDF tool.
note: Current OpenClaw docs still expose config requirements, broader secret coverage, and PDF tooling. Methods: openclaw-configuration:direct-fetch, openclaw-secrets:direct-fetch, openclaw-pdf:direct-fetch.

anchor: OpenClaw can use a managed browser, attached Chrome relay, and remote nodes; headless mode can trigger more anti-bot friction.

anchor: Automated self-update is possible but not the primary recommended operating pattern.
note: The latest FAQ needs a human confirmation of the self-update guidance. Methods: openclaw-faq:direct-fetch.

anchor: Wise personal tokens and OAuth 2.0 support different automation realities, and EU or UK PSD2 rules constrain some personal-token actions.

anchor: Steel Cloud and Steel Local differ materially; auth-sensitive credential injection is not a safe assumption in Steel Local.
